channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Entrepreneurs fight pile-up of garbage in the West Bank
item.description: Israeli restrictions on movement in the West Bank are impeding garbage trucks from reaching landfills, leaving Palestinians living amid mounds of trash. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to change this.
item.content:encoded: Israeli restrictions on movement in the West Bank are impeding garbage trucks from reaching landfills, leaving Palestinians living amid mounds of trash. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to change this.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Life Kit team shares tax choices that save you money
item.description: Life Kit has tips to help you make better tax choices now that'll save you money in 2026.
item.content:encoded: Life Kit has tips to help you make better tax choices now that'll save you money in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: A journalist revisits a decision to report on a teacher for sexual abuse
item.description: Investigative journalist Isolde Raftery of KUOW explains why, two decades later, she re-investigated her own reporting on a teacher accused of sexual abuse.
item.content:encoded: Investigative journalist Isolde Raftery of KUOW explains why, two decades later, she re-investigated her own reporting on a teacher accused of sexual abuse.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: As the Devil Wears Prada 2 struts into theaters, NPR staffers discuss fashion in film
item.description: NPR's fashionistas talk about how their favorite films have influenced what we wear, and the power of costumes to transform character.
item.content:encoded: NPR's fashionistas talk about how their favorite films have influenced what we wear, and the power of costumes to transform character.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Blasting beats and neon lights: inside Nairobi's 'nganya' bus culture
item.description: In Nairobi, the matatu isn't just a ride—it's a rolling nightclub, art gallery, and heartbeat of the city all in one.
item.content:encoded: In Nairobi, the matatu isn't just a ride—it's a rolling nightclub, art gallery, and heartbeat of the city all in one.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: Composer Adrian Younge opens up about making music with a cinematic feel
item.description: Composer Adrian Younge talks about being inspired by the spy-thriller sound of 60s and 70s jazz, what it's like being sampled and why his music has a cinematic feel.
item.content:encoded: Composer Adrian Younge talks about being inspired by the spy-thriller sound of 60s and 70s jazz, what it's like being sampled and why his music has a cinematic feel.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: Modern economic chokepoints in war and the impact on geopolitics
item.description: Hoover Institution fellow Eyck Freymann explains what the Iran war and the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz reveals about modern economic chokepoints, geopolitics and war strategies.
item.content:encoded: Hoover Institution fellow Eyck Freymann explains what the Iran war and the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz reveals about modern economic chokepoints, geopolitics and war strategies.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: How does diplomacy work during a military deadlock?
item.description: Suzanne DiMaggio, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, explains the nuances of diplomatic moves at a time of military stalemate.
item.content:encoded: Suzanne DiMaggio, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, explains the nuances of diplomatic moves at a time of military stalemate.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Comedian Sheng Wang on finding joy in the mundane
item.description: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.content:encoded: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Oil and gas companies making hay by making plastic?
item.description: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.content:encoded: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee
